_meta.name: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)
_meta.what: The EU's carbon levy on imports of certain carbon-intensive goods. An Indian exporter of covered goods to the EU must report the embedded emissions of those goods and, from the definitive period, see a financial obligation applied at the EU border.
_meta.status_note: This is a readiness check, who is in scope and what to prepare. It is not a CBAM liability calculation; embedded-emissions quantification + the certificate cost is a separate, heavier step.

primary_sources.label: European Commission, Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism
ICAP, EU adopts CBAM simplifications ahead of 2026 compliance

chemicals.note: Covered only if the products exported are fertilisers or hydrogen; other chemicals are out of scope.

power_and_energy.note: Covered for electricity exported into the EU grid; most Indian generators do not export power to the EU.

timeline.event: Transitional period, quarterly reporting of embedded emissions only, no financial charge.
Definitive period begins, importers must be authorised CBAM declarants; embedded emissions must be third-party verified.
A new ~50-tonne/year per-importer de-minimis exempts small importers (Omnibus simplification).
Sale of CBAM certificates begins; first surrender (for 2026 imports) due 30 Sep 2027.
Financial obligation phases in: the payable share of embedded emissions rises from 2.5% (2026) toward 100% (2034).

checklist.task: Confirm whether your exported goods are CBAM-covered
checklist.detail: Map your product HS/CN codes to the six covered goods (cement, iron & steel, aluminium, fertilisers, electricity, hydrogen). Only covered goods entering the EU trigger CBAM.

checklist.task: Identify the EU importer of record
checklist.detail: The CBAM obligation legally sits with the EU importer (the authorised declarant), but they will need your installation-level emissions data, clarify who reports and who supplies the data.
Calculate direct (and, where required, indirect) embedded emissions for each good at the producing installation, per the EU's CBAM methodology, not an industry average where actual data exists.
From 2026, embedded-emissions data must be verified by an EU-accredited verifier; unverified shipments default to high EU benchmark values. Verifier availability in India is limited, start early.
Establish a repeatable process to give your EU importer verified emissions data each quarter, with supporting documentation retained for audit.
